Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Observations from the log: Case 1: RA -> VF -> AE -> RMI -> VF -> AD -> SC -> AC Case 2: RA -> VF -> AE -> AD -> SC -> AC Case 3: RA -> VF -> AE -> RMI -> RMI -> VF -> AD -> SC -> AC We note that: - The process starts with RA, then VF, then AE. - After AE, sometimes we have RMI and then VF again, and sometimes we dont. - In case 3, we have two consecutive RMI activities before VF.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can break down the process as: Step 1: Receive Application (RA) Step 2: Validate Form (VF) Step 3: Assess Eligibility (AE) Step 4: Optionally, we may have a loop of: Request Missing Info (RMI) followed by Validate Form (VF) again. This loop can happen zero or more times (in case 1: once, case 2: zero, case 3: twice but note: in case 3 we have two RMI in a row without VF in between?

Name: Validation - NE
Text: So its not a simple loop of (RMI, VF) because then we would expect VF after each RMI. Alternatively, maybe the process allows multiple RMI without VF in between?

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In case 3, after AE we have two RMI then VF. So the pattern is: after AE, we can have zero or more RMI (without VF in between) and then a VF? But note that in case 1 and 3, after the last RMI, we have a VF. This might indicate that the process allows multiple requests for missing info before a re-validation.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: after AE, we have a decision point. Either we go to AD (if no missing info) or we go to RMI. Then after RMI, we must have VF again to revalidate the form. But in case 3, we have two RMI without VF in between. So the process tree for the part after AE is: X( AD, ->( RMI+, VF ) )

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can model the multiple RMI as: * (RMI, tau) which means: do RMI, then either exit or do tau and then RMI again. Then, after the loop (which gives us one or more RMI), we have a VF. So the missing info subprocess is: ->( * (RMI, tau), VF ) And the overall process after AE is: X( AD, ->( * (RMI, tau), VF ) )

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In case 1, we have one RMI: so the loop * (RMI, tau) would do: RMI, then exit the loop. Then we do VF. In case 3, we have two RMI: the loop * (RMI, tau) would do: RMI, then tau, then RMI, then exit. So this models the multiple RMI without VF in between.

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Let Tail = ->( AD, ->( SC, AC ) ) Then the process tree becomes: -> ( RA, -> ( VF, -> ( AE, X( Tail, ->( * (RMI, tau), ->( VF, Tail ) ) ) ) ) )

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Notice that the sequence AD->SC->AC appears in both branches. We can factor that out. This is more compact.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This tree captures the process as described. Fitness: It can replay all traces in the log. Precision: It does not allow too much extra behavior that is not in the log. Simplicity: It uses the basic operators and is not overly complex.
